techniques.evidence_quote: The threat actor was observed leveraging likely previously compromised credential to access several AWS instances within customer's Private Cloud environment
leveraging likely previously compromised credential to access several AWS instances within customer's Private Cloud environment
staged the Rclone tool 'ProgramData\rclone-v1.69.0-windows-amd64.zip', a command-line program to sync files and directories to and from different cloud storage providers
The earliest signs of attack observed by Darktrace involved the use of two likely compromised credentials to connect to the customer's Virtual Private Network (VPN) environment.
the threat actor continued to conduct network scans using the Nmap tool, operating from both a separate AWS domain controller instance and a newly joined device on the network
around 5 GB of data downloaded from an AWS file server

Unusual internal RDP connections to a likely AWS printer instance indicated that the threat actor was looking to strengthen their foothold within the environment and/or attempting to pivot to other devices
the threat actor was further observed accessing and downloading multiple files hosted on an AWS file server instance, notably finance and investment-related files
the PKI-related EC2 instance started making SSH connections with the Rclone SSH client 'SSH-2.0-rclone/v1.69.0' to a RockHoster Virtual Private Server (VPS) endpoint (193.242.184[.]178)

techniques.technique_name: Exfiltration Over Asymmetric Encrypted Non-C2 Protocol
techniques.evidence_quote: The PKI instance continued to make repeated SSH connections attempts to transfer data to this external destination... suggesting the threat actor was exfiltrating the gathered data using the Rclone utility

attack_chain.description: Threat actor used two previously compromised credentials to authenticate to the customer's VPN and gain access to their AWS Virtual Private Cloud environment.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The earliest signs of attack observed by Darktrace involved the use of two likely compromised credentials to connect to the customer's Virtual Private Network (VPN) environment.

attack_chain.description: Once inside, the attacker staged the Rclone tool (rclone-v1.69.0-windows-amd64.zip) onto a PKI-associated AWS EC2 instance under ProgramData, preparing it for use in data exfiltration.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: staged the Rclone tool 'ProgramData\rclone-v1.69.0-windows-amd64.zip', a command-line program to sync files and directories to and from different cloud storage providers, to an AWS instance whose hostname is associated with a public key infrastructure (PKI) service.

attack_chain.description: The attacker accessed and downloaded multiple finance and investment-related files hosted on an AWS file server instance, gathering data prior to exfiltration.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the threat actor was further observed accessing and downloading multiple files hosted on an AWS file server instance, notably finance and investment-related files. This likely represented data gathering prior to exfiltration.

attack_chain.technique_name: Exfiltration Over Asymmetric Encrypted Non-C2 Protocol
attack_chain.description: The PKI EC2 instance used the Rclone SSH client (SSH-2.0-rclone/v1.69.0) to initiate repeated SSH connections to a RockHoster VPS endpoint (193.242.184[.]178), exfiltrating the gathered data over SSH.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the PKI-related EC2 instance started making SSH connections with the Rclone SSH client 'SSH-2.0-rclone/v1.69.0' to a RockHoster Virtual Private Server (VPS) endpoint (193.242.184[.]178), suggesting the threat actor was exfiltrating the gathered data using the Rclone utility they had previously installed.

attack_chain.description: After initial exfiltration was blocked by Darktrace's Autonomous Response, the attacker continued reconnaissance by conducting network scans using Nmap from both an AWS domain controller instance and a newly joined device.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the threat actor continued to conduct network scans using the Nmap tool, operating from both a separate AWS domain controller instance and a newly joined device on the network.

attack_chain.description: Following the scan, the attacker downloaded approximately 5 GB of additional data from an AWS file server as part of continued internal data gathering.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: further internal data gathering activities, with around 5 GB of data downloaded from an AWS file server.

attack_chain.description: The threat actor made unusual internal RDP connections to an AWS printer instance in an attempt to pivot to other devices and strengthen their foothold, likely in response to being blocked by Autonomous Response.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Unusual internal RDP connections to a likely AWS printer instance indicated that the threat actor was looking to strengthen their foothold within the environment and/or attempting to pivot to other devices, likely in response to being hindered by Autonomous Response actions.

attack_chain.technique_name: Exfiltration Over Asymmetric Encrypted Non-C2 Protocol
attack_chain.description: After connections to the original exfiltration endpoint were blocked, the attacker pivoted to use the AWS printer instance to initiate SSH communication with an alternative Host Department VPS endpoint (67.217.57[.]252), previously associated with Akira ransomware SSH-based exfiltration.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Darktrace subsequently observed the AWS printer instance initiating SSH communication with a rare external endpoint associated with the web hosting and VPS provider Host Department (67.217.57[.]252), suggesting that the threat actor was attempting to exfiltrate data to an alternative endpoint after connections to the original destination had been blocked.

initial_access_vector: Compromised credentials used to authenticate via VPN into the customer's AWS Virtual Private Cloud environment
persistence_mechanism: Continued access maintained via lateral RDP movement to additional EC2 instances; fallback exfiltration endpoints used when initial C2 was blocked
privilege_escalation: Not explicitly described; attacker likely already had sufficient access via compromised credentials
